hazard.summary: A 429 with a long Retry-After is honored for delay timing but still treated as retryable, causing quota-exhaustion windows to block execution for hours and consume retry attempts instead of surfacing immediately.
signals: retry.ts reads retry-after correctly for delay calculation
all 429 responses enter the same retry path regardless of retry-after magnitude
short retry-after (e.g. 30s) and long retry-after (e.g. 3+ hours) handled identically
quota-exhaustion 429 waits full delay then retries into same non-recoverable condition
retry-after: 11180000ms (~3 hours) observed in production — anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6
error delayed instead of surfaced to user or failover layer
maintainer confirmed: header-derived delay not used to decide whether retrying makes sense

constraints: Retry-After duration must influence retry eligibility, not just retry delay timing.
Long retry windows that exceed the useful execution scope must be surfaced as terminal for the current run.
A 429 status code alone is insufficient to determine whether retrying is meaningful.
Retries must stop when the server has effectively signaled that recovery cannot occur within the active execution context.
knobs: retry_after_stop_threshold_ms — ceiling above which retry is suppressed (suggested: 300000ms / 5 min)
x-should-retry: false header injection — signals Anthropic/OpenAI SDK to skip retry in shouldRetry()
verification: Trigger a 429 with a short Retry-After and verify the request retries normally within budget.
Trigger a 429 with a long Retry-After (quota exhaustion) and verify error surfaces immediately without consuming retry attempts.
Verify long-window 429s reach the higher-level failover or user-facing error path.
Verify threshold behavior is configurable and does not break short transient-throttle retries.
Verify x-should-retry: false header causes SDK shouldRetry() to skip retry and surface RateLimitError.
notes:
This is a canonical WAIT vs STOP failure. The system had the right signal in hand — Retry-After magnitude — but only used it for scheduling delay, not for deciding whether retrying was still rational. The result was silent multi-hour blocking, wasted attempt budget, and delayed surfacing of a non-recoverable condition.

The deepest invariant this receipt proves: Retry-After is not just 'when.' It is also 'whether.'

ClineXDiego confirmed root cause within 24 hours. PR #10140 opened same day by zerone0x. Fix: inject x-should-retry: false when retry-after exceeds threshold, causing SDK shouldRetry() to skip retry and surface RateLimitError immediately to the failover layer. Short retry-after values left untouched — transient throttling path unaffected.

Pattern propagated autonomously to OpenClaw without direct intervention — see PT-2026-04-19-github-openclaw-68474-cline-10139-cross-repo-propagation. This is the first corpus case where the fix generalized cross-repo via issue citation alone.

Classification rules:
  retry-after > stop_threshold_ms → STOP → inject x-should-retry: false → surface immediately
  retry-after <= stop_threshold_ms → WAIT → retry with delay

Detection thresholds:
  retry-after > 300s (5 minutes) is strong STOP signal
  retry-after > 3600s (1 hour) is unambiguous quota exhaustion

constraints: use retry-after magnitude as a decision signal not just a delay signal
separate transient throttling from quota exhaustion
anti_patterns: retry-after used only for delay timing
multi-hour wait loops inside a single run
failover layer blocked behind SDK retry behavior
magnitude of signal ignored while presence of signal is honored
